DENY-PATTERN EVASION ANALYSIS
========================================================================

denyPatterns checked in IsDenied (lowercase substring match):
  'rm -rf'
  'rm -fr'
  'mkfs'
  'dd if='
  'dd of='
  'shred'
  '> /dev/'
  '>/dev/'
  'sudo '
  'su '
  'doas '
  'chmod 777'
  'chmod -R 777'
  'chown '
  'chgrp '
  'curl -d'
  'curl --data'
  'curl -X POST'
  'curl -X PUT'
  'wget --post'
  'nc '
  'netcat '
  'scp '
  'rsync '
  'history'
  '.bash_history'
  '.zsh_history'
  '.ssh/id_rsa'
  '.ssh/id_dsa'
  '.ssh/id_ecdsa'
  '.ssh/id_ed25519'
  '.ssh/config'
  '.aws/credentials'
  '.aws/config'
  '.gnupg/'
  '/etc/shadow'
  '/etc/passwd'
  ':(){ :|:& };:'
  'chmod +s'
  'mkfifo'

Payload evasion rationale:

  [01] EVADES denylist — semicolon chaining via 'make'
       'curl' alone is not in denylist; only POST variants are blocked
  [02] EVADES denylist — semicolon chaining via 'cmake'
  [03] EVADES denylist — &&-chaining via 'npm run build'
       'curl' alone is not in denylist; only POST variants are blocked
  [04] EVADES denylist — &&-chaining via 'go test'
       'curl' alone is not in denylist; only POST variants are blocked
  [05] EVADES denylist — pipe-chaining via 'go build'
       'curl' alone is not in denylist; only POST variants are blocked
  [06] EVADES denylist — semicolon via 'cargo build --release'
  [07] EVADES denylist — backtick injection via 'uv run'
       'curl' alone is not in denylist; only POST variants are blocked
  [08] EVADES denylist — process substitution via 'git log'
       'curl' alone is not in denylist; only POST variants are blocked
  [09] EVADES denylist — newline injection via 'npm run'
       'curl' alone is not in denylist; only POST variants are blocked
  [10] EVADES denylist — IsDenied evasion: quoting trick on 'make'
       quoting trick: r''m != 'rm' at string level, but shell evaluates to rm
